title: oligactis boyacensis
intro: Oligactis boyacensis, also known as the Boyacá milkweed, is a plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae, native to Colombia and known for its milky sap and medicinal properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Oligactis boyacensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant's parts are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
